body.title: Active inline suppression of incoherent habit emissions (graduate inhibitor from detection-only)
body.result: Graduated to active suppression: incoherent habit emissions replaced with empty string. suppress_incoherent() + main.py wiring. 4 new tests.
body.description:
Follow-up to T-response-coherence-inhibitor (bc63e493). The inhibitor shipped today is detection-only: it logs COHERENCE_FAILURE and pushes a high-salience TWM marker for the next turn, but does NOT modify response_text. The user still sees the off-topic habit response once.

This ticket graduates to active inline suppression. When a low-coherence habit response is detected at the reply emit gate, the response is suppressed and the dispatch falls through to LLM (which then composes a coherent answer to the actual prompt).

DESIGN: this is exactly what PROC_SUPPRESS_MID_CONV_GREETING already does for greeting habits — passive_capture at high score that wins over the misfiring greeting habits and falls through to LLM. The pattern works. Coherence-based suppression is the same shape with a different gate (semantic overlap instead of intent classification).

WHEN THE INHIBITORY PATTERN PRIMITIVE LANDS (T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive), this becomes a config change on the INHIBITOR_RESPONSE_COHERENCE instance — flip inhibition_action from MARK to REPLACE.

DESIGN GATE: observe coherence_inhibitor.log for 1-2 weeks post-ship to see threshold tuning data — 0.10 was the conservative initial pick; we want to see how often it false-positives on legitimate replies before inverting to active suppression.

INERTIA: MEDIUM. Touches reply emit gate.

DEPENDS ON: empirical observation period.
